channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Makati Run Raises 8,000 for Relief Funds
item.description: The Angat Buhay charity event held in Makati City successfully raised 8,000 pesos to support relief efforts for communities affected by recent disasters. Organized as part of a larger initiative to co...

item.title: Magistrates Seek Judicial Independence and Better Working Conditions
item.description: Magistrates in South Africa are pushing for greater independence from the Department of Justice (DoJ), citing concerns over administrative control, inadequate resources, and disparities in treatment c...

item.title: China Denies US Election Interference Claims, Calls Accusations 'Unfounded'
item.description: China has categorically rejected allegations by former US President Donald Trump that Beijing interfered in the 2024 US elections, describing the claims as 'unfounded' and accusing Washington of launc...

item.title: Vancouver West End Offers Dog-Friendly ATM Service
item.description: A unique service has been introduced in Vancouver’s West End, where an ATM is available for dogs to access cash without requiring a PIN. This initiative, reported by CTV News on July 18, 2026, aims to...

item.title: SRC Approves Salary Increases and New Allowance Structure for Civil Servants
item.description: The Salaries and Remuneration Commission (SRC) has approved revised salary structures and new allowances for national government civil servants, effective from 1 July 2026. The changes cover basic sal...

item.title: Ukrainian Strikes Target Russian Warehouses, Kill Eight
item.description: Ukrainian drones launched explosive attacks on Russian e-commerce warehouses in Moscow and Tambov regions, killing eight workers and causing massive fires. The strikes, part of Kyiv's 'long-range sanc...

item.title: EV Charging Infrastructure Has Improved Dramatically Over Three Years
item.description: The article details a recent 600-mile road trip using an Audi e-tron, highlighting significant improvements in EV charging infrastructure compared to three years ago. The author used ABRP app for rout...

item.title: Medical Certificates Under Scrutiny as Commission Investigates Potential Fraud
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry has raised serious concerns about the validity of medical certificates submitted by witnesses, with calls for stricter oversight. The controversy erupted after Inves...

item.title: Govt Spokesperson Apologizes and Seeks Support After UDA's Ol Kalou By-Election Loss
item.description: Government spokesperson Isaac Mwaura visited Kieni Constituency to appeal for continued support of President William Ruto's administration following the ruling UDA party's heavy defeat in the Ol Kalou...

item.title: NBA Election Disrupted by Cyberattack as Voting Delays Raise Concerns
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) election faced significant disruption due to a coordinated cyberattack that delayed voting by over seven hours. The Electoral Committee of the Nigerian Bar Associati...

item.title: Alleged Insider Trading Allegations Surround Rep. Anna Paulina Luna and MAGA Influencer's Political Bet
item.description: U.S. Representative Anna Paulina Luna of Florida is alleged to have shared confidential information about Donald Trump’s choice of JD Vance as his 2024 vice-presidential running mate with a MAGA influ...

item.title: King David Junior School Founder Ssekade Tadoe Remembered After Fatal Bus Crash Claimed His Life and 20 Pupils
item.description: King David Junior School founder and director Ssekade Tadoe was among the 21 people who lost their lives in a tragic bus crash as the school group returned from an educational trip to Sipi Falls. Acco...

item.title: Tripura police arrest elderly man accused of long-term sexual harassment and suicide abetment
item.description: A 75-year-old man from Tripura's Gomati district was arrested by police on Saturday for allegedly sexually harassing his daughter-in-law over an extended period. The case was registered under the Bhar...

item.title: Spain halts World Cup training over stormy weather; Argentina presses on
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup finalists, Spain and Argentina, faced contrasting weather challenges ahead of the final. Spain canceled their outdoor training session at Melanie Lane in New Jersey due to a se...

item.title: Alouettes defeat Stampeders 38-32 in CFL game
item.description: The Montreal Alouettes secured a 38-32 victory over the Calgary Stampeders in a thrilling CFL matchup. Quarterback Davis Alexander set a league record with 336 consecutive passes without an intercepti...

item.title: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested by federal authorities in Miami, according to the US Marshals Service. The warrant for their arrest was sealed, with no further details provid...

item.title: ABS-CBN Announces Word of the Day: 'Garuk' for July 19, 2026
item.description: The article announces the Word of the Day for July 19, 2026, which is 'garuk'. This initiative by ABS-CBN aims to educate the public on the meaning and usage of the word, promoting linguistic awarenes...

item.title: England Defeats France 6-4 in High-Scoring World Cup Third-Place Match
item.description: In a historic and thrilling conclusion to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, England secured third place with a dramatic 6-4 victory over France in Miami Gardens, Florida. Bukayo Saka emerged as the star of the...

item.title: Chelsea signs Morgan Rogers in record £117m deal after Villa Europa League success
item.description: Chelsea FC has agreed to pay a record £117m fee to Aston Villa for the transfer of Morgan Rogers, marking the biggest deal in club history. The midfielder, who led Aston Villa to Europa League glory l...

item.title: Ex-Tory MP calls for balanced security measures amid political threats and violence
item.description: The article discusses the need for security for Members of Parliament (MPs) in the UK, highlighting historical and contemporary threats to their safety. Ex-Tory MP Jerry Hayes recounts his personal ex...
